2604.21496.title: How English Print Media Frames Human-Elephant Conflicts in India
2604.21496.paper_summary: Human-elephant conflict (HEC) is rising across India as habitat loss and expanding human settlements force elephants into closer contact with people. While the ecological drivers of conflict are well-studied, how the news media portrays them remains largely unexplored. This work presents the first large-scale computational analysis of media framing of HEC in India, examining 1,968 full-length news articles consisting of 28,986 sentences, from a major English-language outlet published between January 2022 and September 2025. Using a multi-model sentiment framework that combines long-context transformers, large language models, and a domain-specific Negative Elephant Portrayal Lexicon, we quantify sentiment, extract rationale sentences, and identify linguistic patterns that contribute to negative portrayals of elephants. Our findings reveal a dominance of fear-inducing and aggression-related language. Since the media framing can shape public attitudes toward wildlife and conservation policy, such narratives risk reinforcing public hostility and undermining coexistence efforts. By providing a transparent, scalable methodology and releasing all resources through an anonymized repository, this study highlights how Web-scale text analysis can support responsible wildlife reporting and promote socially beneficial media practices.
2604.21496.paper_summary_zh: 人象衝突（HEC）在印度日益加劇，因為棲息地喪失和人類定居點擴張迫使大象與人類的接觸更為密切。雖然衝突的生態驅動因素已被廣泛研究，但新聞媒體如何描繪這些衝突仍然很少被探討。本研究呈現了對印度HEC媒體框架的首次大規模計算分析，檢視了2022年1月至2025年9月期間，來自一家主要英語媒體的1,968篇完整新聞文章，總共包含28,986句。利用結合長上下文Transformer、大型語言模型和特定領域的負面大象描繪詞彙的多模型情感框架，我們量化情感、提取理由句，並識別促成對大象負面描繪的語言模式。我們的研究結果顯示，恐懼引發和與攻擊相關的語言佔主導地位。由於媒體框架可以塑造公眾對野生動物和保護政策的態度，這類敘事風險加強公眾的敵意，並削弱共存努力。通過提供透明、可擴展的方法論並通過匿名存儲庫釋放所有資源，本研究突顯了網絡規模文本分析如何支持負責任的野生動物報導並促進社會有益的媒體實踐。
2604.21496.author: Bonala Sai Punith et.al.
2604.21496.authors: Bonala Sai Punith, Salveru Jayati, Garima Shakya, Shubham Kumar Nigam
